title: Changes in Neural Connectivity and Memory Following a Yoga Intervention for Older Adults: A Pilot Study
headline: Changes in Neural Connectivity and Memory Following a Yoga Intervention for Older Adults: A Pilot Study
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined brain & mechanisms. No study has explored the effect of yoga on cognitive decline and resting-state functional connectivity.
keyFindings: No study has explored the effect of yoga on cognitive decline and resting-state functional connectivity.

This study explored the relationship between performance on memory tests and resting-state functional connectivity before and after a yoga intervention versus active control for subjects with mild cognitive impairment (MCI).
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Changes in Neural Connectivity and Memory Following a Yoga Intervention for Older Adults: A Pilot Study
aiSnippet: RCT, Journal of Alzheimer's disease : JAD (2016). No study has explored the effect of yoga on cognitive decline and resting-state functional connectivity. This study explored the relationship between performance on memory tests and resting-state functional connectivity before and after a yoga intervention versus active control for subjects with mild cognitive impairment (MCI).
mechanismGap: The practice in this study does not share a specific breath-regulation or directed-attention mechanism with the Miracle of Mind practice, so no practice bridge is drawn here.
